## What Changed

- Removed `headed` and `engine` controls from the MCP tool surface for the public docs and research web workflow
- Hardened the MCP bridge so managed browsing arguments are rejected explicitly instead of being forwarded silently
- Reframed MCP instructions around the recommended evidence loop: `tb_search -> tb_search_open_top -> tb_read_view -> tb_extract`
- Added `mainContentReason`-aware guidance so poor main-only captures explain the failure mode instead of nudging the model toward headed retries
- Updated MCP smoke coverage and CLI stream assertions to match the new headless-only public research contract

## User Impact

- MCP clients now get a narrower, more reliable research-oriented contract instead of a browser-debugging surface
- Search engine selection stays automatic in MCP, which reduces host-specific prompt burden and removes one common misuse path
- When a page needs challenge, auth, or MFA recovery, MCP now points callers toward supervised recovery instead of exposing browser-mode escape hatches

## Notes

- This release follows `v0.1.6` and keeps the same tag-driven standalone bundle publishing workflow
- The official install and update path still uses the GitHub Release bundle for the matching release tag
- The product direction for MCP is now explicitly `public docs / research web`, not a general-purpose remote browser control surface
